claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask: **Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. Alex lives with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great. Now I need to ask: **Are you responsible for Alex's care? For example, do you provide for his food, clothes, and other needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for all of his care - I provide his food, clothes, everything he needs day to day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Has anyone else already claimed Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One final question: **Are you a UK resident with the right to reside in the UK, or do you have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK resident with the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You live with him in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for his care and provide for all his needs
- You are a UK resident with the right to reside
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit. You can now proceed to make a claim for Alex if you haven't already done so.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can use the Child Benefit tax calculator to work out if this applies to you.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for his care, providing food, clothes, and all his day-to-day needs. This satisfies the requirement that the claimant is responsible for the child and lives with them. The claimant is a UK resident with the right to reside in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the key requirements: Alex is under 16, the claimant lives with Alex in the UK, they are responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, they are a UK resident with the right to reside, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for his care, providing food, clothes, and all his day-to-day needs. This satisfies the requirement that the claimant is responsible for the child and lives with them. The claimant is a UK resident with the right to reside in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the key requirements: Alex is under 16, the claimant lives with Alex in the UK, they are responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, they are a UK resident with the right to reside, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
